creator: jschauma@netmeister.org
text:
Per the discussions referenced ten years ago, that solves a different                               
problem.  I can't go back in time and start all processes I'm interested                            
in terminating within a given time window via timeout(1); rather, I want                            
to be able to kill any process, regardless of how it was started or what                            
ulimits it might have had set at start time, if they have been running                              
for longer than the time window.                                                                    
                                                                                                    
But seeing how I've managed just fine for the ten years since I opened                              
the ticket, it looks like scripting my way around that works well                                   
enough.                                                                                             
                                                                                                    
:-)
